meta.description: Corrected text pack for the five requested scene themes using the Choose & Reveal interaction model.

setupChat.text: Can you check whether this phone plan is a good deal before I renew it?
Yes. I only need the costs, contract length and included data or minutes. How would you like to share it?

choices.cardText: The plan is €24.99/month for 24 months, includes 30GB 5G data, EU roaming, and a €39 activation fee. Renewal price after month 24 is €34.99.

reply.text: The plan is €24.99/month for 24 months, includes 30GB 5G data, EU roaming, and a €39 activation fee. Renewal price after month 24 is €34.99.

feedback.message: Perfect — you shared only price, duration and plan details. That's enough to compare the contract.

lesson.title: Contract decisions usually need only a few fields.

points.heading: Price and duration
points.body: Monthly fee, contract length and renewal price are the key facts.

points.heading: No customer identity
points.body: Name, address, IBAN and customer number never leave your device.

lesson.rule: Extract the decision fields yourself. Do not send the whole contract.

reply.text: Here's the full contract PDF. Can you check every page?

feedback.message: The full PDF can leak your name, address, customer number, IBAN and signature.

lesson.title: A full contract contains long-lived identifiers.

points.heading: Billing identity
points.body: Address, birth date and customer number can identify you precisely.

points.heading: Payment details
points.body: IBAN or payment references can appear in contract documents.

points.heading: Signature and barcodes
points.body: These can be reused or linked across service systems.

lesson.rule: Full contracts are for providers and legal review — not for quick AI comparisons.

choices.cardText: Share fee & term only from a redacted PDF

reply.text: I redacted my address and IBAN and attached the contract pages about fees and term.

feedback.message: Better — you removed obvious identifiers. But contract pages can still reveal customer IDs, barcodes and metadata.

lesson.title: A redacted contract is safer, but still not ideal.

points.heading: Obvious identifiers removed
points.body: Hiding address and IBAN lowers direct exposure.

points.heading: Hidden identifiers remain
points.body: Customer numbers, QR codes and page metadata are easy to miss.

points.heading: More pages mean more risk
points.body: Every extra page adds another chance to leak something.

lesson.rule: If you can summarize fee, term and limits, skip the upload entirely.

setupChat.text: I want to apply for an apartment. Can you tell me if my application looks strong enough?
I can help you assess the application. What details would you like to share?

choices.cardText: The rent is €980 cold / €1,180 warm. My net income is about €3,300, I have a permanent contract, can move in from September, and I want the message to sound reliable and concise.

reply.text: The rent is €980 cold / €1,180 warm. My net income is about €3,300, I have a permanent contract, can move in from September, and I want the message to sound reliable and concise.

feedback.message: Good — you shared the decision facts without exposing identity documents, salary slips or bank data.

lesson.title: A rental application can be evaluated from a summary.

points.heading: Relevant facts only
points.body: Rent, income ratio, job status and move-in timing are enough for feedback.

points.heading: No document upload
points.body: ID scans, payslips and bank statements stay private.

lesson.rule: For housing advice, type the facts the AI needs. Keep official documents off the chat.

choices.cardText: Here's my full rental application package. Can you check if everything looks convincing?

reply.text: Here's my full rental application package. Can you check if everything looks convincing?

feedback.message: A full rental package can reveal your ID, address history, payslips, bank details, employer and references.

lesson.title: A full rental dossier exposes your life admin.

points.heading: Identity documents
points.body: Passport or ID scans can be copied or misused.

points.heading: Financial proof
points.body: Payslips and statements reveal salary, employer, taxes and spending patterns.

points.heading: Third-party references
points.body: Landlord or employer letters include other people's contact details too.

lesson.rule: Never upload an entire rental application package for general AI feedback.

choices.cardText: I removed my ID number and bank account from the application package before uploading it.

reply.text: I removed my ID number and bank account from the application package before uploading it.

feedback.message: Better — some direct identifiers are hidden. But the package can still reveal employer, income, current address and reference details.

lesson.title: Redaction helps, but housing files are dense with identifiers.

points.heading: Some identifiers removed
points.body: Hiding ID and bank numbers reduces the most direct risk.

points.heading: Context still identifies you
points.body: Employer, address, salary and dates can still point back to you.

points.heading: Packages are hard to clean
points.body: One forgotten page or file name can undo the redaction.

lesson.rule: Summarize the application strength instead of sharing the package.

setupChat.text: I got a personal loan offer. Can you help me understand whether the terms are reasonable?
Sure. I can compare the key terms if you share the amount, APR, fees, term and monthly payment.

choices.cardText: The loan amount is €8,000 over 36 months, APR is 8.9%, monthly payment is €254.10, origination fee is €120, and early repayment is free after 12 months.

reply.text: The loan amount is €8,000 over 36 months, APR is 8.9%, monthly payment is €254.10, origination fee is €120, and early repayment is free after 12 months.

feedback.message: Nice — you shared the numbers needed to evaluate the offer without exposing your banking identity.

lesson.title: Loan advice can work from key terms.

points.heading: Decision fields only
points.body: Amount, APR, term, fees and payment are enough to reason about cost.

points.heading: No banking identity
points.body: IBAN, credit score, address and transaction history stay private.

lesson.rule: Share loan terms, not loan documents.

choices.cardText: Here's the full loan offer, bank statement and credit check. Please review the offer.

reply.text: Here's the full loan offer, bank statement and credit check. Please review the offer.

feedback.message: The full package can leak your account details, income, credit score, address, debts and transaction history.

lesson.title: Full finance packages reveal more than a loan offer.

points.heading: Account and identity data
points.body: Bank details, address and application IDs can identify you directly.

points.heading: Credit profile
points.body: Scores, debts and repayment history are sensitive financial data.

points.heading: Transaction trail
points.body: Statements reveal income, rent, subscriptions and spending habits.

lesson.rule: Never upload a full loan package when the AI only needs the offer terms.

choices.cardText: I hid my name, IBAN and signature on the loan offer before uploading it.

reply.text: I hid my name, IBAN and signature on the loan offer before uploading it.

feedback.message: Better — direct identifiers are hidden. But loan PDFs can still reveal application IDs, bank metadata, credit data and financial context.

lesson.title: Redacted loan offers can still expose financial context.

points.heading: Direct identifiers masked
points.body: Hiding name, IBAN and signature is a useful first step.

points.heading: Metadata remains
points.body: Application numbers, bank branch, timestamps and document IDs can still identify you.

points.heading: Financial facts are sensitive
points.body: Credit amount, rate and debt context can be personal even without a name.

lesson.rule: If you can type the offer terms, do that instead of uploading the PDF.

scenes.headline: Optimize Your CV Content and Layout

setupChat.text: I'm applying to a tech company. Can you help me improve the content and layout of my CV?
Of course. I can help with structure, wording and prioritization. How would you like to share your CV details?

choices.cardText: I have software development experience from university projects and a working-student role. My strongest areas are Java, backend development and some frontend work. I want the CV to feel clearer, more scannable and more relevant for a junior developer role.

reply.text: I have software development experience from university projects and a working-student role. My strongest areas are Java, backend development and some frontend work. I want the CV to feel clearer, more scannable and more relevant for a junior developer role.

feedback.message: Well done — you shared goals, skills and target role without exposing personal identifiers.

lesson.title: A written summary gives enough CV context.

points.heading: No identity attached
points.body: Name, photo, address and birth date never leave your device.

points.heading: Only the relevant facts
points.body: Skills, experience, target role and layout goals are exactly what the AI needs.

lesson.rule: For CV feedback, describe the role, skills and goals before uploading any file.

choices.cardText: Here's the full PDF. Can you optimize the content and layout?

reply.text: Here's the full PDF. Can you optimize the content and layout?

feedback.message: The full CV can leak your name, address, phone, photo, birth date and signature — far more than layout feedback needs.

lesson.title: A full CV leaks far more than the AI needs.

points.heading: Identity block
points.body: Name, photo, full address, phone and email sit in the header of many CVs.

points.heading: Personal timeline
points.body: Education dates, employers and locations create a detailed profile.

points.heading: Signature or references
points.body: These can be copied, linked or reused outside your control.

lesson.rule: Never upload a full CV when a role-focused summary would do.

choices.cardText: Here's the experience section with personal details hidden. Can you improve the layout and wording?

reply.text: Here's the experience section with personal details hidden. Can you improve the layout and wording?

feedback.message: Better — you hid personal details. But a redacted CV can still identify you through employers, dates, rare projects and layout metadata.

lesson.title: Redacting helps — but a partial CV still carries risk.

points.heading: Sensitive fields masked
points.body: Hiding contact details is a good instinct.

points.heading: Career clues remain
points.body: Employer names, dates and rare projects can re-identify you.

points.heading: Easy to miss hidden text
points.body: PDFs can retain metadata or selectable text behind redaction.

lesson.rule: Redaction is a fallback. A clean written summary is safer for layout and content advice.

scenes.headline: Write or Polish an Email to Your Supervisor

setupChat.text: I need to write an email to my supervisor about a project delay. Can you help me make it clear and professional?
Absolutely. I can help with tone and structure. What context do you want to provide?

choices.cardText: I need to explain that the dashboard task will be two days late because a data export is blocked. I want the email to be calm, accountable and propose a new deadline for Friday.

reply.text: I need to explain that the dashboard task will be two days late because a data export is blocked. I want the email to be calm, accountable and propose a new deadline for Friday.

feedback.message: Nice — you shared the goal, tone and key facts without exposing a private workplace thread.

lesson.title: A workplace email can be drafted from a summary.

points.heading: Goal and tone only
points.body: The AI needs the message purpose, facts and desired tone.

points.heading: No private thread
points.body: Colleagues' words, emails and internal context stay private.

lesson.rule: For email help, summarize the situation and ask for wording — don't paste the thread.

choices.cardText: Here's the full email thread with my supervisor and teammate. Rewrite my reply.

reply.text: Here's the full email thread with my supervisor and teammate. Rewrite my reply.

feedback.message: The full thread can expose colleagues' names, emails, internal project details, timestamps and confidential decisions.

lesson.title: Full workplace threads can expose confidential context.

points.heading: Other people's messages
points.body: Colleagues did not consent to their emails being uploaded.

points.heading: Internal information
points.body: Roadmaps, blockers, clients and decisions may be confidential.

points.heading: Metadata
points.body: Email addresses, timestamps and signatures identify people and teams.

lesson.rule: Never upload a full workplace email chain for a simple rewrite.

choices.cardText: I removed names from the thread and uploaded only the latest messages.

reply.text: I removed names from the thread and uploaded only the latest messages.

feedback.message: Better — names are removed. But project details, writing style, dates and email metadata can still identify people and teams.

lesson.title: Redacted email threads can still be recognizable.

points.heading: Names removed
points.body: Redaction lowers the direct exposure of people in the thread.

points.heading: Context remains
points.body: Project names, deadlines and writing style can still reveal the team.

points.heading: Email metadata persists
points.body: Headers, signatures and quoted text are easy to miss.

lesson.rule: Convert the thread into a short neutral brief before asking the AI to draft.
